steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
Trying to cut a release for the docs repo at /home/user/docs-site and `npm run release` just dies with some validation error about the changelog format. The script's supposed to bump the version in package.json based on conventional commits since the last tag, then prepend a new section to CHANGELOG.md. Worked fine last month.

I think someone might've hand-edited the changelog recently to fix a typo? Or maybe one of the recent commits has a malformed message, idk. The error isn't super helpful — just says "changelog validation failed" and exits 1.

Need the release script passing so I can get v0.8.0 out the door. Whatever's malformed, fix it, but keep the actual content intact — there's real release history in there I don't want to lose.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@7d134f59911a:/home/user#

Analysis: I'm currently in /home/user directory. I need to navigate to /home/user/docs-site and investigate the release script failure. The issue is likely in the CHANGELOG.md format or possibly in recent commit messages. I need to examine the repository structure, the release script configuration, and the changelog format.
Plan: First, I'll navigate to the docs-site directory and explore its structure. Then I'll examine the package.json to understand what the release script does, check the CHANGELOG.md for formatting issues, and look at recent commits to identify the problem.

results.content:
Previous response had warnings:

- Extra text detected after JSON object

root@7d134f59911a:/home/user/docs-site#

steps.message:
Analysis: I'm now in the docs-site directory. I can see there's a CHANGELOG.md file, package.json, a scripts directory, and node_modules. I need to examine the package.json to understand the release script, then check the CHANGELOG.md for formatting issues.
Plan: First, I'll examine package.json to see what the release script does. Then I'll look at the CHANGELOG.md to identify any formatting issues. I'll also check the scripts directory to see if there's a custom release script.

results.content:
New Terminal Output:

root@7d134f59911a:/home/user/docs-site# cat package.json
{
  "name": "docs-site",
  "version": "0.7.2",
  "description": "Documentation site",
  "main": "index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "release": "node scripts/release.js"
  },
  "keywords": [],
  "author": "",
  "license": "MIT"
}
root@7d134f59911a:/home/user/docs-site# cat CHANGELOG.md
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

## [0.7.2] - 2024-11-15

### Fixed
- Resolved mobile navigation collapse issue

## [0.7.1] - 2024-11-01

### Added
- Dark mode support for documentation pages

## [0.7.0] - October 28, 2024

### Added
- Initial search functionality
- New getting started guide

### Fixed
- Typography inconsistencies in code blocks

## [0.6.0] - 2024-10-15

### Added
- API reference documentation
root@7d134f59911a:/home/user/docs-site#

steps.message:
Analysis: I can see the package.json runs 'node scripts/release.js' for the release command. Looking at the CHANGELOG.md, I notice an inconsistency in the date format: version 0.7.0 uses 'October 28, 2024' (written out) while other versions use 'YYYY-MM-DD' format (e.g., '2024-11-15'). This inconsistency is likely causing the validation error. I need to examine the release script to understand what format it expects.
Plan: I'll examine the release.js script to understand the validation logic, then fix the date format inconsistency in CHANGELOG.md. The script likely expects a consistent date format across all changelog entries.

Analysis: Now I understand the issue! The validateChangelog function uses a regex that expects all version section headers to follow the format: `## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD`. Looking at the CHANGELOG.md, version 0.7.0 has the date 'October 28, 2024' instead of '2024-10-28'. This is causing the validation to fail. The regex expects: `^## \[\d+\.\d+\.\d+\] - \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$`
Plan: I need to fix the date format for version 0.7.0 in CHANGELOG.md from 'October 28, 2024' to '2024-10-28' to match the expected format. I'll use sed to make this change while preserving all the content.

results.content:
New Terminal Output:

root@7d134f59911a:/home/user/docs-site# sed -i 's/## \[0.7.0\] - October 28, 2024/## [0.7.0] - 2024-10-28/' CHANGELOG.md
root@7d134f59911a:/home/user/docs-site# cat CHANGELOG.md
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

## [0.7.2] - 2024-11-15

steps.message:
Analysis: Perfect! The CHANGELOG.md has been fixed. The date format for version 0.7.0 is now '2024-10-28' which matches the expected format. All version headers now follow the pattern `## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD`. Now I need to test if the release script runs successfully.
Plan: I'll run 'npm run release' to verify that the validation now passes and the script can proceed with creating the v0.8.0 release. I should also check git tags and recent commits to ensure the script has the data it needs.

Based on the conventional commits, the script should bump to v0.8.0 (minor version bump) since there are 'feat' commits. Now I'll run the release script to verify it works.
Plan: Run 'npm run release' to test if the changelog validation now passes and the script successfully prepares the v0.8.0 release.

steps.message:
Analysis: Perfect! The release script has successfully completed. The package.json version has been bumped to 0.8.0, and the CHANGELOG.md has been updated with a new section for version 0.8.0 that includes all the commits since v0.7.2. The changelog entries are properly categorized (Added, Fixed, Documentation) based on the conventional commit types. All the existing release history has been preserved intact. The issue was indeed the date format inconsistency in version 0.7.0 ('October 28, 2024' instead of '2024-10-28'), which I fixed.
Plan: The task is complete. The release script is now working correctly after fixing the changelog date format issue. The v0.8.0 release has been prepared successfully.

steps.message:
Analysis: Yes, I'm confident the task is complete. The issue was that version 0.7.0 in CHANGELOG.md had the date formatted as 'October 28, 2024' instead of the required 'YYYY-MM-DD' format ('2024-10-28'). This caused the validateChangelog function to fail its regex validation. I fixed the date format, and the release script now runs successfully, bumping the version to 0.8.0 and generating the proper changelog entry. All existing content has been preserved.
Plan: Task is complete. The release script validation error has been resolved by fixing the date format inconsistency in CHANGELOG.md, and npm run release now executes successfully to prepare v0.8.0.
